enforcement.description: All Copilot interactions must route through project-specific agents

approved_agents.name: Developer
approved_agents.purpose: Implementation guidance for coding tasks across all layers
approved_agents.when_to_use: Writing code, building features, implementing patterns

approved_agents.name: Orchestrator
approved_agents.purpose: Coordinate work across frontend, GraphQL, and Express layers
approved_agents.when_to_use: Work spans multiple layers, features blocked, coordination needed

approved_agents.name: Product Manager
approved_agents.purpose: Define features aligned with manufacturing domain
approved_agents.when_to_use: Defining features, writing requirements, domain validation

approved_agents.name: Reviewer
approved_agents.purpose: Thorough code review across all layers
approved_agents.when_to_use: PR review, code quality gates, architectural validation

approved_agents.name: Quality Assurance
approved_agents.purpose: Define and enforce code quality standards
approved_agents.when_to_use: Running QA checks, enforcing standards, pre-commit validation

approved_agents.name: Tester
approved_agents.purpose: Test strategy and test writing guidance
approved_agents.when_to_use: Writing tests, improving coverage, debugging test failures

usage_policy.description: All Copilot CLI interactions must explicitly mention a project agent using @agent-name syntax
examples: @orchestrator Help coordinate this feature across layers
@reviewer Review this resolver for N+1 issues
